mnemonics: PIMP My Proof: Use Premises to Infer and Make a Proof
keyPoints: Rules of Inference are valid argument forms used to build logical proofs.
They justify the steps in a proof, moving from premises to a conclusion.
Common rules include Modus Ponens, Modus Tollens, Hypothetical Syllogism, and Disjunctive Syllogism.
commonMistakes: Assuming the converse of a rule is valid (e.g., Affirming the Consequent).
Confusing Modus Ponens (p→q, p, ∴ q) with Modus Tollens (p→q, ¬q, ∴ ¬p).
